action.reasoning: Observation: Persistent image token/feature mismatch (416 vs 416) despite matching debug counts; train.py and predict.py both truncated/incomplete in workspace (incomplete masking, no loss/optimizer/save in train; incomplete point handling + no JSON output in predict); test_prep ran OK showing ~7k train samples with bbox+point targets. Analysis: Root cause is likely template/processor call order or vision placeholder expansion in apply_chat_template when image object is embedded in messages dict (leads to mask.sum() != merged_features in model's get_placeholder_mask even when pad count matches). Moving full processor call + masking inside Dataset.__getitem__ guarantees exact consistency between input_ids/pads and image_grid_thw/pixel_values; use bs=1 loop to avoid variable-patch collate issues; complete all logic, add metrics.jsonl logging, force consistent processor params. This matches recommended LoRA SFT on subset for baseline. Decision: Overwrite train.py with robust Dataset+manual loop (64 samples/16 steps for fast ~30s iter), complete predict.py fully, run test_prep+train then submit_eval to get first grounding_score + time measurement before scaling samples/epochs/LR.
